title: Hotels in rural areas of Shivamogga badly hit due to disruption in supply of commercial LPG
content: Many small hotel owners, who usually earn few thousand rupees a day, have been forced to shut down their businesses, due to non-availability of LPG

title: Sathya Sai district police issue stern warning against cricket betting
content: The Superintendent of Police cautioned that those turning cricket into a form of gambling and exploiting innocent people will face firm action

title: Indian Railways shows red flag to unauthorised expenses
content: The Railway Board racks the whip after audit reports pointed to huge unsanctioned expenditure in the last 5 years

title: Modi targets LDF, UDF over decades of corruption, calls BJP Kerala’s real A-team
content: Positioning the BJP-led NDA as a decisive alternative, the Prime Minister said that an NDA government would advance the vision of a “Viksit Kerala

title: Viral Bengaluru Central Prison video may be staged; probe points to smear campaign
content: Three inmates have been placed in quarantine cells for disciplinary reasons; Officials stated that the inmates confessed that the video was intended to frame prison authorities in retaliation for stricter rules

title: Incarceration without trial is punishment, says Supreme Court; grants bail to accused in jail for two years
content: A bench of Justices Dipankar Datta and P.V. Varale, in its recent order, said Pradeep Kumar alias Banu was booked for various offences, including attempt to murder, in February 2024, but the prosecution is yet to examine any one of the 23 witnesses related to the case

title: Closure of eateries: Migrant labourers return to their native States
content: Lakhs of workers were engaged in hotels, restaurants, roadside eateries, catering and fast food centres in State

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Ballot printing only in 3 govt presses this time as ECI introduces colour images
content: Of the 11 presses under the department in the State, the ballots for the upcoming polls can be printed only at two facilities in Thiruvananthapuram and one in Ernakulam

title: Amit Shah says curbing ‘infiltration not enough’, every illegal immigrant must be deported from Assam
content: Claiming that "Assam State Congress president Gaurav Gogoi wants to protect infiltrators", Amit Shah asked him to clarify his stand on the issue.

title: Four children of a single family drowned in farm pond in Yadgir district
content: Initial reports suggest that all children were gone for swimming and drowned one after another without knowing the depth of the pond

title: Breaks ensured I stay fresh and excited: Kohli
content: Virat Kohli's 38-ball 69, studded with five sixes and as many fours, powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a comfortable six-wicket win over Sunrisers Hyderabad in the IPL season opener on March 28

title: Nashik court extends 'godman' Ashok Kharat's police custody till April 1
content: Ten FIRs, eight for alleged sexual assault or exploitation and two for cheating, have so far been registered against him at Sarkarwada police station in the city.

title: BJP to score hat-trick in Assam, to come to power for third term in row: Nitin Nabin
content: The BJP president Nabin also attacked the previous Congress governments for allegedly engaged in vote bank politics instead of developing the State

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: PM targets LDF, UDF in Kerala, says BJP will transform State with ‘Modi guarantees’
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, in a rally in Kerala's Palakkad, said the BJP will transform Kerala into a developed State, and the State will move forward on the strength of Modi's guarantees.

title: Mann Ki Baat: Chhattisgarh farmers’ water conservation efforts earn praise; Vishnu Deo Sai calls it proud moment
content: Chhattisgarh Chief Minister, who listened to the broadcast at his official residence, noted that the indigenous 'Aawa Pani Jhonki' model has become a national benchmark for grassroots innovation

title: Government to fill up 8,000 vacant posts in police department this year: Siddaramaiah
content: Calling upon the police officials to serve the society with integrity and honesty, Mr Siddaramaiah said the officers need not worry about their family members while they are on duty.

title: AIADMK expels Dusi K. Mohan after TVK announces his candidature in Cheyyar
content: A communication from AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami said Mr. Mohan's actions violated party discipline and brought disrepute to the organisation

title: Mysuru airport announces revised flight schedules
content: The update effective from March 29 as part of the Summer Schedule aims to streamline operations and enhance passenger connectivity

title: Mann ki Baat: Don’t politicise the challenges created by West Asian crisis, urges PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Modi also talked about India’s various cricketing achievements in the recent past, and a number of initiatives being taken up across the country towards nation-building

title: Class four student murdered, grandmother injured in attack
content: The boy’s grandmother, G. Lakshmi (68), suffered critical injuries and was admitted to a hospital in Vijayawada

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Full list of LDF candidates
content: The Kerala Legislative Assembly elections are scheduled to take place on April 9, 2026 and will be held in a single phase to select 140 members of the Assembly

title: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin to begin his election campaign in Tiruvarur on March 31
content: Mr. Stalin will address a meeting on South Street near the Tiruvarur Thyagarajaswamy Temple and cover the Mannargudi, Tiruvarur, Kizhvelur, Nannilam and Thiruthuraipoondi constituencies on the same day.

title: Strengthening of Penna River flood wall essential: Nellore MP, MLA tell Amit Shah
content: The leaders presented the need to fortify the existing walls and to form new bunds wherever necessary to prevent the raging river from gushing into the adjoining fields during the monsoon season

title: Kerala Assembly elections 2026: All three candidates sound confident as campaign trail heats up along coastal Kochi
content: UDF’s Mohammed Shiyas warns that past elections were ignored lessons for the Left front, with voters now set to oust them; LDF’s K.J. Maxy emphasises a decade of development work; NDA’s Xavier Julappan spotlights unimplemented local projects

title: NIMHANS study flags rising parental concern over children’s screen time
content: ‘Parents are increasingly reporting difficulties in regulating their children’s technology use, especially when it begins to interfere with sleep, studies and daily routines’

title: T.N. Assembly election: AMMK manifesto seeks Bharat Ratna for Muthuramalinga Thevar, Jayalalithaa
content: Seeking the waiver of crop loans taken by farmers from cooperative and nationalised banks, the AMMK also called for the expansion of the protected agricultural zone.

title: TVK chief Vijay to contest from Perambur, Tiruchi East constituencies making electoral debut in T.N. Assembly election
content: TVK general secretary ‘Bussy’ N. Anand will contest from T. Nagar in Chennai, while K.A. Sengottaiyan will test the waters from his home turf, Gobichettipalayam. Aadhav Arjuna will contest from Villivakkam, K.G. Arunraaj from Tiruchengode and C.T.R. Nirmal Kumar from Thirupparankundram.

title: Tracing Puducherry’s voting pattern in the past three electoral battles
content: The AINRC remains the mainstay of the Union Territory’s political landscape, a position once occupied by the Congress. The decision of businessman Jose Charles Martin — who has deep pockets — to venture into electoral politics may have changed the dynamics of the 2026 election in Puducherry

title: ACB traps Panchayat Raj AEE, Works Inspector in Vizianagaram
content: The accused reportedly demanded a ₹1.30 lakh bribe to clear the pending bills for the construction works taken up under the Gram Panchayat General Funds and the 15th Finance Commission Fund

title: Energy price surge poses new challenges for India’s real estate sector
content: Rising crude oil and natural gas costs are increasing construction expenses and disrupting material supply, creating uncertainty for developers and homebuyers

title: Defamation case against Sobha Surendran posted to October 24
content: The case stems from statements allegedly made by her claiming that E.P. Jayarajan, a central committee member of the CPI(M), had considered joining the BJP

title: As parties intensify efforts to woo voters, ‘power art’ takes centre stage in Chennai
content: The lace garlands woven by the artisans in Chintadripet take on a new character ahead of elections, writes R. Ragu

title: High up, and soaring: Chennai’s rental market
content: From core city neighbourhoods to fast-growing suburbs, Chennai’s rental market is witnessing a sharp upswing. Nearly 49% of the households in the city still depend on rental accommodation, data show. More often than not, tenants are forced to compromise on space and budget. This report presents a list of emerging rental trends

title: 2 Indian, 1 Pakistani ships cross Strait of Hormuz
content: Two other LPG carriers belonging to the state-owned SCI had crossed the strait and have discharged their cargo in India.

title: 40 pilgrims injured as bus rams into tree in Uttar Pradesh's Mau
content: The accident occurred around 1.30 a.m. on the Ratanpura-Mubarakpur road under the Haldharpur police station area when the driver lost control of the bus and rammed it into a roadside tree, police said.

title: West Asia crisis: Rajnath Singh calls for swift decisions, long-term readiness
content: Mr. Singh made the remarks while chairing the first meeting of the Informal Group of Ministers (IGoM) set up to monitor the situation in West Asia

title: CRPF officer who lost leg in line of duty battling pain, stagnant career
content: Ajay Malik, an Assistant Commandant with CRPF, lost his leg after he stepped on an IED in Jharkhand forest; retired CAPF personnel say officers face career stagnation though they work under grave conditions

title: Why is there controversy over the Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill?
content: Why was the Bill introduced in the Rajya Sabha? Why is there opposition to it? What is the government’s justification?

title: Kerala Assembly Polls 2026: Inflation, a major talking point ahead of polls
content: The spike in food prices has emerged as a major concern for the public. A long-standing welfare architecture in Kerala has attempted to cushion the effects of inflation, especially for the vulnerable. However, a few experts have raised doubts about the effectiveness of the State government’s market intervention measures

title: NMC launches online feedback system for undergraduate medical students
content: Anonymous student feedback form to assess medical college infrastructure, teaching quality and clinical training

title: Centre continues to be evasive on AIIMS for Kerala
content: Responding to questions posed by Kodikunnil Suresh and Adoor Prakash in the Lok Sabha, Union Minister of Health said “the proposal for establishment of AIIMS in Kerala has not been approved” and provided no concrete timeline for future approval

title: What did the Supreme Court rule on Scheduled Castes and religion?
content: What did the Supreme Court say about Scheduled Caste status for those not belonging to Hinduism, Sikhism, or Buddhism? Can SC status be restored through reconversion? What is the Centre’s position on SC status for Dalit Muslims and Christians? What about Scheduled Tribes?

title: News in Frames: Licensed to bloom
content: Farming communities in Madhya Pradesh and Rajasthan cultivate opium, a practice passed down for generations and tied to tradition and prestige alike

title: $3 billion toll: US counts losses in aircraft, drones, defence systems
content: Iran's war has severely impacted US military assets, with initial losses and repairs estimated between $1.4 billion and $2.9 billion. Ballistic missiles and drone strikes have targeted advanced fighter jets, refueling tankers, and crucial surveillance systems, including expensive radars.  The conflict's financial toll is escalating, even without a full ground war.

title: 'Remember these two criminals': Iran names US officers responsible for Minab school strike
content: Iran has identified two US Navy officers from the USS Spruance as responsible for a missile strike on a Minab school that killed 168 people. The strike, part of Operation Epic Fury, targeted a nearby IRGC naval base. While US media suggests the school was damaged in coordinated operations, a preliminary US investigation indicates a potential targeting error.

title: 'Ek ko papa chun le': Harbhajan's reply to ‘three clowns’ jibe goes viral
content: Harbhajan Singh went viral after hitting back at a troll who called him, Virender Sehwag, and Aakash Chopra “three clowns” during an IPL broadcast. He replied, “Inme se ek ko papa chun le,” earning praise for his witty comeback. The exchange sparked debate on trolling, highlighting how quickly social media reactions can escalate during major cricket events.

title: US man sells home for $1 million using ChatGPT, reveals what got highest return
content: A Miami man successfully sold his house for nearly $1 million by leveraging ChatGPT for the entire process, from pricing and marketing to negotiations. The AI tool even advised on cost-effective home improvements, leading to a sale price significantly higher than local agent estimates. While the AI guided most steps, a lawyer was still consulted for final paperwork.

title: 'A long wait of nearly seven decades': PM Modi hails J&K's Ranji win
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hailed Jammu and Kashmir’s first Ranji Trophy win as a proud moment after nearly 70 years. In ‘Mann Ki Baat’, he praised the team’s hard work, captain Paras Dogra’s leadership, and Aaqib Nabi’s 60 wickets. Modi said the victory has inspired the region and boosted sports enthusiasm, adding that Jammu and Kashmir is emerging as a sporting hub and expressing hope for continued success.

title: OpenAI founder proposes to remove income tax on Americans earning less than $100k
content: Venture capitalist Vinod Khosla proposes eliminating federal income tax for Americans earning under $100,000, funded by higher capital gains taxes. He believes this addresses AI-driven job loss fears, a major concern for voters. Khosla also critiques current political approaches to AI, seeking a "surprise" presidential candidate focused on displaced worker security.

title: Up, up and away: Is the world running out of Helium gas?
content: Global helium supplies face a critical shortage due to Middle East tensions disrupting production and shipping. This invisible gas, vital for MRIs, semiconductors, and advanced tech, is irreplaceable and lost forever once released. India, heavily reliant on imports, sees soaring prices and potential disruptions in healthcare and its burgeoning chip industry.

title: ‘Bizarre at this age’: Ashwin in awe of Virat Kohli’s relentless intensity
content: Ravichandran Ashwin lauded Virat Kohli's "bizarre" energy levels at his age, stating the Indian stalwart continues to "walk the talk" after stepping away from Tests and T20Is. Kohli's unbeaten 69 in the IPL 2026 opener against SRH showcased his enduring hunger and intensity between the wickets, setting a benchmark for the game.

title: India at 116: Why India’s growth isn’t translating into happiness
content: Despite India's rapid economic growth, the World Happiness Report ranks it low, highlighting that material progress alone doesn't ensure well-being. Factors like urbanization pressures, job insecurity, and evolving social structures contribute to lower life satisfaction, underscoring the need for a broader definition of progress beyond GDP.

title: Asia’s largest marble waste dumping yard in Rajasthan draws visitors
content: Asia's largest marble waste dumping site in Kishangarh, Rajasthan, has transformed into a popular tourist spot, attracting thousands daily for its striking white landscape and photo opportunities. Despite its scenic appeal, environmentalists warn of severe health and ecological hazards due to unregulated dumping and contamination.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' BO day 11: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 800 crore net
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' is making history with every passing day at the Indian box office. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, the espionage thriller has crossed Rs. 790 crore in India net after eleven days, becoming only the third Hindi film ever to breach the Rs. 800 crore domestic net mark. The film is now chasing the lifetime record of its predecessor, 'Dhurandhar,' which stands at Rs. 894.49 crore — a milestone that could fall within the current week. Globally, the film is tracking past Rs. 1,079 crore and closing in on Rs. 1,200 crore worldwide, which would place it among the top three highest-grossing Indian films of all time.

title: Virat Kohli 'changed a lot after retiring from Test cricket'
content: Virat Kohli’s unbeaten 69 powered RCB to a successful chase, showing his top form after a T20 break. Dale Steyn praised his improved six-hitting and evolving mindset. Kohli’s aggressive approach has grown over time. Devdutt Padikkal said Kohli’s presence eased pressure, while also crediting coaches Andy Flower and Dinesh Karthik for his development.

title: 'BJP says can't eat fish, meat': Mamta Banerjee campaigns in Purulia
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee fiercely criticized the BJP in Purulia, accusing them of intending to curb local food habits like fish and meat consumption. She also alleged the Election Commission's voter list deletions targeted her supporters and highlighted the success of her Lakshmir Bhandar scheme, warning of its discontinuation under BJP rule.

title: Bigg Boss 18 fame Rajat Dalal gets married; shares a glimpse of his wedding ceremony
content: Bigg Boss 18 contestant Rajat Dalal has announced his marriage, marking a new chapter in his life. He expressed gratitude to his fans for their unwavering love and support, acknowledging their role in his journey. Dalal's fans have responded with congratulations and well wishes for his married life.

title: Bihar Board 10th result 2026: What to do if you fail in BSEB matric exam
content: The Bihar Board has declared the Class 10 Result 2026 with an 81.79 percent pass rate, but students who failed still have options. Candidates can apply for compartment exams, special exams, or scrutiny between April 1 and April 7. The Board aims to release compartment results by May 31, allowing students to continue their studies without losing a year. These measures ensure multiple opportunities for students to improve their performance.

title: MI vs KKR: 5 players to watch out for
content: Mumbai Indians face Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL 2026 opener at Wankhede. MI aim to end their title drought, while KKR seek a comeback. Key players include Rohit Sharma, Suryakumar Yadav, and Mitchell Santner for MI, alongside Sunil Narine and Blessing Muzarabani for KKR, who could play decisive roles in this high-stakes clash.

title: Foreign investors stay on selling spree, pull out record Rs 1.14 lakh cr in March
content: Foreign investors withdrew a record Rs 1.14 lakh crore from Indian equities in March, driven by Middle East tensions, rupee pressure, and concerns over high crude oil prices impacting growth. This significant outflow follows a period of strong inflows in February and reflects a broader risk aversion trend in emerging markets.

title: Hormuz chokehold: What's happening to petrol prices in India & neighbouring nations?
content: Global crude markets are strained as Middle East conflict escalates, pushing oil prices up and impacting fuel supplies worldwide. Countries like India, Nepal, Bhutan, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Myanmar, and Bangladesh are implementing measures such as price adjustments, import increases, and conservation efforts to manage the crisis.

title: Gurgaon horror: Speeding Thar ploughs into man, 2 grandsons, all dead; chilling CCTV emerges
content: A tragic accident in Pataudi claimed the lives of a 60-year-old man and his two grandsons, aged 8 and 10, when a Mahindra Thar struck them at high speed. The victims were on the roadside when the SUV rammed them, causing fatal injuries. The driver, an IT firm employee, was arrested following the incident.

title: 12-year-old girl steals candy box from shop, father beats her to death in UP
content: A father allegedly beat his 12-year-old daughter to death in Bulandshahr. The girl reportedly stole candies from a local shop. Her mother was injured while trying to intervene. The father is accused of murder and efforts are underway to apprehend him. The incident occurred after a Navratri feast.

title: Mumbai Indians hit 300-match mark, first franchise to reach T20 milestone
content: Mumbai Indians are set to become the first franchise team to play 300 T20 matches when they face Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL 2026 opener. This historic milestone underscores their enduring presence and success in the shortest format of the game. The team aims to mark this occasion by ending their five-year title drought.

title: ‘Targeted assasination’: Iran blasts Israel killing 3 journalists; questions int'l law
content: Three journalists were killed in an Israeli strike in southern Lebanon, sparking outrage from Iran and press watchdogs. While Israel labeled one victim a "terrorist," Lebanon's presidency condemned the attack as a "blatant crime" violating international law. The Committee to Protect Journalists is investigating, noting a disturbing pattern of accusations against media workers without credible evidence.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: TVK chief Vijay to contest from 2 seats, Perambur and Trichy East
content: TVK chief Vijay will contest the upcoming Tamil Nadu polls from Perambur and Trichy East. The party has also announced its candidates for several other key constituencies, including T Nagar, Villivakkam, Gobichettipalayam, Tiruchengode, Tiruparankundram, Kolathur, and Egmore, as it gears up for the elections.

title: ‘Two-year ban not working’: Sunil Gavaskar calls for stricter IPL pullout rules
content: Sunil Gavaskar has urged the BCCI to implement tougher penalties for last-minute IPL withdrawals, citing Ben Duckett's exit as evidence the current two-year ban is ineffective. Duckett's decision to prioritize England commitments, despite being picked by Delhi Capitals, has disrupted team planning and reignited calls for greater player accountability in the league.

title: BSEB Class 10th Results: 81.79% students pass, Pushpanjali Kumari and Parveen secure top rank
content: The Bihar School Examination Board has declared the Class 10 Matric Result 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 81.79 percent. Pushpanjali Kumari and Parveen jointly secured the top rank with 492 marks (98.4 percent). The results were announced during an official press conference attended by key officials. Over 1.5 million students appeared for the examination. Students can check their results online, while the Class 11 admission process is set to begin in early April.

title: 'Return as captain': Virat Kohli urged to rethink Test retirement
content: Virat Kohli starred with an unbeaten 69, guiding Bengaluru to a record chase of 202 against Hyderabad. Ambati Rayudu said he’s far from a finished product and should return to Tests. Hyderabad posted 201/9, but Kohli, with Padikkal and Patidar, sealed the fastest 200+ IPL chase while crossing 4000 runs in chases and surpassing Shoaib Malik.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ makes over Rs 1,200 crore worldwide in 10 days
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is performing strongly at the box office, crossing Rs 1,200 crore worldwide after a 10-day run. The action-thriller saw a significant surge on its second Saturday, contributing to its impressive domestic and global collections. The film continues the story of an undercover agent battling terrorism.

title: Gujarat woman strangled with clutch wire by ex-domestic help she trusted for 15 years
content: A 36-year-old woman in Palanpur was brutally murdered by a trusted associate over a dispute involving gold, silver jewellery, and cash. The victim had previously lent valuables and money to the accused, who allegedly conspired with her husband and two others to kill her and dispose of the body.

title: 'Petrol, diesel crisis developing worldwide': PM Modi cautions during Mann ki Baat
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi warned of a global petrol and diesel shortage due to ongoing conflicts impacting vital energy regions. Speaking on Mann Ki Baat, he highlighted how recent wars, particularly in the Gulf, are disrupting energy supplies.  He expressed gratitude to Gulf nations for assisting over a crore Indians residing there amidst the escalating tensions.

title: ‘Put down the crown, clown’: Thousands rally against Trump in 'No Kings' protests
content: Thousands of people joined “No Kings” protests across the United States and parts of Europe, opposing the war in Iran and the policies of Donald Trump. Minnesota became the focal point, with a major rally in St Paul led by Bruce Springsteen. While most protests were peaceful, some clashes were reported, and Republican leaders dismissed the demonstrations as politically motivated.

title: Virat Kohli surpasses Pakistan batter to become Asia’s top T20 run-scorer
content: Virat Kohli has etched his name in T20 history, becoming the Asian batter with the most runs, surpassing Shoaib Malik.  Achieving this feat in the IPL opener, Kohli's unbeaten 69 guided RCB to victory. He attributed his sustained performance to careful workload management and a commitment to team success, highlighting his enduring impact on the game.

title: Bengaluru horror: Man slits throats of mother, sister, nephew, then attempts suicide
content: A family of four attempted suicide due to mounting debts, resulting in two deaths and two critically injured. Preliminary investigations suggest financial distress from a chit fund business and a daughter's medical treatment led to the tragedy. The family recorded a video before the incident, citing pressure from lenders.

title: ‘We weren’t disciplined’: Vettori comes down hard on SRH bowlers after loss to RCB
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad head coach Daniel Vettori described his team's bowling effort in their IPL 2026 opener as "one of our poorer performances" after a six-wicket loss to Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Despite posting 201/9, SRH struggled to contain RCB's batting, with Vettori citing missed lengths and a lack of discipline as key issues.

title: Anushka Sharma’s floral top and retro denims steal the show at IPL 2026 opener
content: Anushka Sharma captivated at IPL 2026, pairing an ₹18K floral Sézane blouse with relaxed bell-bottom denims. This chic, high-low mix exuded effortless French-girl style and everyday comfort. Her natural beauty and candid reactions, alongside Virat Kohli's winning gesture, made for viral moments, highlighting a modern fashion trend towards authenticity and ease.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: DMK releases list of 164 candidates, fields more than 60 new faces
content: In a bold move ahead of the elections, DMK has rolled out its roster of 164 candidates, blending experienced stalwarts with more than 60 fresh entrants eager to make their mark. Chief Minister M.K. Stalin championed the 'mega mega alliance' during the campaign kickoff, setting sights on the western region.

title: 'Virat Kohli was over': Mohammad Kaif's post after RCB win goes viral
content: Two years after retiring from T20Is, Virat Kohli continues to shine, scoring an unbeaten 69 to guide Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a big win over Sunrisers Hyderabad. Mohammad Kaif praised his form. Kohli also broke records, becoming the first to 4000 IPL chase runs, as RCB sealed a dominant victory.

title: ‘Targets around globe’: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un oversees high-thrust solid-fuel rocket engine test
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un oversaw a test of a high-thrust solid-fuel rocket engine as part of the country’s defence plan, according to Korean Central News Agency. The development highlights efforts to strengthen missile capabilities, with analysts saying the improved engine could support long-range weapons, including intercontinental ballistic missiles, and enhance strike capacity.

title: Bengaluru Metro services disrupted on Purple Line for maintenance
content: The temporary halt disrupted early morning commuters travelling along the busy Purple Line corridor, as the maintenance announcement came only on Sunday morning when the work began, leaving passengers with no time to alter their plans or arrange alternative modes of transport.

title: Prevent allotment of reserved election symbols of one party to a member of another party, urges a PIL plea before Madras High Court
content: Litigant says, it has become a common practice among members of political parties to contest in reserved symbols of alliance partners

title: How Arizona beat Purdue and Illinois defeated Iowa in Saturday's Elite Eight
content: The Wildcats and Fighting Illini are Final Four bound. Here's how they punched their tickets to Indianapolis.

title: No. 1 Arizona into first Final Four since 2001
content: The No. 1 seed Arizona Wildcats are into their first Final Four since 2001 after beating No. 2 Purdue 76-64.

title: Adesanya walloped by Pyfer in 2nd-round finish
content: Joe Pyfer scored the biggest win of his career Saturday night, finishing two-time champion Israel Adesanya by ground-and-pound in Round 2 in the UFC Fight Night main event.

title: Braves' Smith hits walk-off days after mom's death
content: Making his Braves debut, Dominic Smith said he "got choked up" thinking about his late mother as he celebrated his walk-off grand slam in Saturday's 6-2 win over the Royals.

title: Verstappen considering quitting F1 at end of 2026
content: Max Verstappen has said he is considering retiring from Formula 1 at the end of 2026, admitting he is "not enjoying the sport"

title: Illinois coach Underwood fulfills Final Four 'dream'
content: Keaton Wagler scored 25 points as Illinois beat Iowa on Saturday, sending the program to the Final Four for the first time in 21 years.

title: USMNT routed by Belgium in blow to WCup prep
content: The USMNT suffered a setback in its preparations for the World Cup as Belgium ran rampant in the second half to emerge with a 5-2 win at Atlanta's Mercedes-Benz Stadium on Saturday.

title: Easily out, L.A.'s Freeman tagged by Marte's hug
content: With nowhere to run on the basepaths, the Dodgers' Freddie Freeman walked toward a smiling Ketel Marte, who tagged him out with a hug to end the second inning of Saturday night's game.

title: Mayweather pumps brakes on Pacquiao rematch
content: Floyd Mayweather cast some doubt on details of his pending fight with Manny Pacquiao, saying the location has not be set and that the bout will actually just be an exhibition.

title: 📸 LeBron to Bronny: Father-son assist makes history
content: The father-son duo etched their names in the history books after the elder James found his son for a 3-pointer.

title: Women's March Madness 2026: Ranking the Elite Eight
content: All four No. 1 seeds advanced to the regional final. The ACC, Big Ten and SEC each have two teams in the field.

title: Japanese Grand Prix: Kimi Antonelli takes second career win in Suzuka - as it happened
content: The third grand prix of the 2026 Formula 1 season is upon us at the Suzuka Circuit in Japan, and you can follow it all with ESPN.

title: NBA playoff watch: With postseason teams set, seeding battles begin
content: Things are getting tight in the East. Plus, updated playoff projections, draft lottery standings after Saturday.

title: Predicting top wide receiver prospects' NFL stats: Who ranks No. 1 in the 2026 draft class?
content: Which receivers in the 2026 draft class will have the most success in the pros? We have projections for the top prospects.

title: Latest NFL draft buzz: Ty Simpson's pro day and more
content: How high could QB Ty Simpson get picked? Is Jeremiyah Love really a possibility for the Titans at No. 4?

title: USMNT handed reality check by Doku, Belgium ahead of World Cup
content: Belgium provided a sobering assessment of where the USMNT currently stands, doling out a heavy 5-2 defeat that will leave Mauricio Pochettino with plenty to ponder.

title: Player ratings: Weah poor as USMNT suffer heavy Belgium defeat
content: It was a difficult game for the U.S. against Belgium, with Tim Weah struggling to make a positive impact.

title: Man United need to decide soon whether to back Carrick or hire someone else
content: Man United are approaching the point at which they need to decide: is Michael Carrick our next permanent manager or not? Ian Darke unpacks their dilemma.

title: Time is running out for Foden to make England's World Cup squad
content: In less than 18 months, the Man City playmaker has gone from England mainstay to the fringe of the squad.

title: WNBA's CBA: 10 biggest wins from new agreement
content: The CBA permanently and positively changes the WNBA's landscape. These are the wins, biggest strides and areas of compromise.

title: Lehigh Valley IronPigs poke fun at Zack Wheeler's rib removal in rehab promo
content: The Phillies' ace had a rib removed as part of a surgery to remove a blood clot -- and kept it in his closet.

title: Moses Itauma isn't the next heavyweight star, his era is already here
content: Moses Itauma's emphatic rise up the heavyweight pecking order continued on Saturday as the boxing world watched in awe at the 21-year-old's knockout over Jermaine Franklin Jr.

title: Tyrell Fortune: Living to fight another day after they pronounced me dead
content: Bellator heavyweight Tyrell Fortune recalls the weight cut that nearly took his life and left him with a new outlook.
